lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: Good morning everyone, Q4 was a milestone quarter for TechVenture as we achieved record ARR growth of twenty-two percent.

lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: While our top-line momentum remains strong, we are maintaining a disciplined stance on regional operating expenses to protect our long-term scalability.

lines.speaker: CFO
lines.text: Turning to the numbers, our EBITDA margins faced some compression this quarter, landing at fourteen percent due to front-loaded infrastructure investments.

lines.speaker: CFO
lines.text: Gross retention remains healthy at ninety-one percent, though we did see some elongation in mid-market sales cycles through December.

lines.speaker: Analyst (Morgan Stanley)
lines.text: Thanks for the color, can you bridge the gap between that record ARR and the softer-than-expected revenue guidance for Q1?

lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: We believe the market is stabilizing, but we’re being prudent with guidance given the ongoing budget scrutiny our clients are facing.

lines.speaker: Analyst (Morgan Stanley)
lines.text: Follow-up for the CFO, your CAC jumped nearly fifteen percent this quarter; is that the new baseline or a one-off spike?

lines.speaker: CFO
lines.text: That increase reflects higher bidding costs in the enterprise segment, so we are trending toward a more elevated acquisition cost in the near term.

lines.speaker: Analyst (Goldman Sachs)
lines.text: On the competitive front, how are you defending your pricing power against the aggressive discounting we’re seeing from legacy players?

lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: We don't compete on price alone, though we have occasionally offered flexible payment terms to secure strategic multi-year renewals.

lines.speaker: Analyst (Goldman Sachs)
lines.text: But if margins are already pressured, doesn't that flexibility threaten your path to free cash flow positive by 2025?

lines.speaker: CFO
lines.text: I want to be clear that our path to profitability is intact, subject to market conditions remaining relatively stable next year.

lines.speaker: Analyst (Goldman Sachs)
lines.text: And what about the churn in the SMB segment? It seems higher than the historical average you mentioned earlier.

lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: The SMB space is certainly more volatile right now, and we are strategically pivoting our sales focus toward more resilient enterprise accounts.

lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: We are confident that this shift will normalize our churn rates as we move into the second half of 2025.

lines.speaker: CFO
lines.text: To add to that, we’re strictly managing our headcount growth to offset any further margin volatility from the SMB side.

lines.speaker: Analyst (Morgan Stanley)
lines.text: Understood, so it sounds like growth is the priority even if it means some short-term pain on the bottom line?

lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: Our priority is sustainable growth, even if that requires us to be more aggressive in defending our market share against new entrants.

lines.speaker: CFO
lines.text: We’ll provide more granular detail on the 2025 roadmap during our next filing, but for now, we remain cautiously optimistic.

lines.speaker: CEO
lines.text: Thank you all for joining us; we look forward to executing on these strategic initiatives in the coming months.
